content:
{
  "title": "Program Sketch Prompting: Enhancing Code Generation via Structured Outline Guidance",
  "problem": "Current prompting methods for code generation often yield semantically incorrect or inefficient code due to ambiguous task descriptions. There is a need for prompting strategies that better guide LLMs toward accurate and efficient program synthesis.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly deployed for code assistance, improving the controllability and correctness of their outputs is crucial for developer productivity and software reliability. Recent advances in prompting have not fully leveraged high-level structural hints that human programmers use. Prompting methods that incorporate explicit program sketches could bridge this gap, enabling LLMs to better understand intent and constraints.",
  "method": "We propose 'Program Sketch Prompting', where the prompt includes not only the natural language specification but also an explicit, partial program outline (sketch) with placeholders. The LLM is instructed to fill in the placeholders to produce complete, functional code. Sketches can specify function signatures, control flows, or key variable initializations, providing structure without dictating full details. We design an automatic sketch extraction and insertion pipeline to generate sketches from target code for training and evaluation prompts.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on HumanEval and MBPP, comparing our method to standard text-only and ICL prompting baselines. Metrics will include functional correctness (pass@k), code efficiency, and syntactic accuracy. Ablation studies will test the impact of sketch granularity, and user studies will assess perceived controllability and ease-of-use."
}
